services.metaTitle: Computer Diagnostics for European Cars | Tony's, Alexandria
services.metaDescription: Warning light on your BMW, Mercedes, Audi or Porsche? Tony's European Auto in Alexandria reads every module with dealer-grade tools. Call (318) 445-6007.
services.tagline: Dealer-grade scan tools that read your whole car, with answers explained in plain English.
intro: A modern European car is a network of computers. The engine talks to the transmission, the transmission talks to the ABS, and a dozen body modules talk to each other over the same bus. When a warning light comes on, a generic code reader from the parts store sees almost none of that. It pulls a single number and leaves you guessing.

We use dealer-grade scan tools that read faults across every system on the car: engine, transmission, ABS, airbags, climate, and the body modules most shops can't even see. Then we tell you in plain language what the car is actually reporting, what it means, and what it will take to fix. No mystery, no upsell built on a code you can't read yourself.

included.title: Full-car module scan
included.detail: We read fault codes from every controller on the vehicle, not just the engine. That means transmission, ABS, SRS airbag, body, comfort, and infotainment modules are all checked so nothing hides behind a single light.

included.title: Live data and sensor analysis
included.detail: We watch the car's sensors in real time while it runs: fuel trims, misfire counts, mass airflow, wheel speeds, and more. Live data is how you tell a failing sensor from a failing part, instead of replacing both.

included.title: Electrical and wiring diagnosis
included.detail: European electrical gremlins (a draw that kills the battery overnight, a module that won't wake up, a circuit dropping out) get traced to the actual fault. We follow the wiring, not the guesswork.

included.title: Intermittent-fault chasing
included.detail: The hardest problems are the ones that come and go. We pull freeze-frame data captured when the fault occurred and test under the conditions that trigger it, so a problem that 'never does it at the shop' still gets found.

included.title: Warning light decoding
included.detail: Check engine, ABS, airbag, EPC, drivetrain malfunction, tire pressure: we explain exactly what each light on your dash is telling you and how urgent it really is.

included.title: A clear, honest plan
included.detail: You leave knowing the root cause, the recommended repair, and the cost. If the fix is simple, we tell you. If it can wait, we tell you that too.

signals: Check engine, EPC, or drivetrain malfunction light is on
A warning light comes and goes for no clear reason
Rough idle, hesitation, or a sudden drop in power
The battery keeps dying overnight or the car won't wake up
A shop already replaced parts and the light came right back
services.whyEuropean: European cars hide most of their diagnostic information behind manufacturer-specific systems a generic scanner never reaches. A BMW throwing a drivetrain malfunction, a Mercedes with a phantom airbag light, an Audi flagging a misfire from carbon buildup, or a VW with a CAN bus fault all need tools that speak the car's own language. A general shop reads the one code it can see and starts swapping parts. We read the whole car, find the actual cause, and save you from paying for the wrong fix twice.

process.detail: Phone us at (318) 445-6007 and tell us what the car is doing and which lights are on. We'll get you scheduled, Monday through Friday.
We connect dealer-grade tools, read every module, watch live data, and trace the fault to its root cause instead of the nearest symptom.
We walk you through what we found in plain English, what it costs to fix, and what's safe to wait on. The decision stays yours.

faqs.q: My check engine light is on but the car drives fine. Do I still need a scan?
faqs.a: Yes. A car can run normally while a sensor or emissions component is failing, and a small fault left alone often turns into an expensive one. A scan tells you whether it's minor or urgent before it becomes a bigger repair.

faqs.q: Another shop already read my codes. Why is yours different?
faqs.a: Most generic readers only see engine codes and give a vague description. We read every module on the car and use live data to confirm the actual cause, so you're not paying to replace a part that was never the problem.

faqs.q: Can you find a problem that only happens sometimes?
faqs.a: Often, yes. The car stores freeze-frame data from the moment a fault occurs, and we test under the conditions that trigger it. Intermittent electrical and sensor faults are exactly the kind of work we specialize in.

services.metaTitle: Factory-Spec Fluid Service | Tony's European Auto, Alexandria
services.metaDescription: Oil, transmission, coolant and brake fluid done to your marque's exact spec at Tony's European Auto in Alexandria, LA. Call (318) 445-6007 to book.
services.tagline: The right fluid, to the right specification, for your European car: not whatever a quick-lube has on the shelf.
intro: On a BMW, Mercedes, Porsche or Audi, fluids are not a commodity. The wrong oil viscosity, a coolant that is not on the approved list, or a transmission fill done by guesswork can cost you a timing chain, a head gasket, or a gearbox. The factory wrote a specification for a reason, and we follow it.

We service every fluid in your car to the manufacturer's exact requirement and we tell you the truth about what actually needs doing. Family-owned in Alexandria for 30 years, with the dealer-grade knowledge to back it up. Call us to book.

included.title: Oil changes to marque spec
included.detail: The correct full-synthetic and viscosity for your engine, with an oil-spec approval the factory recognizes (for example the LL or MB-Approval ratings). On Mercedes we check the oil filter housing for the common gasket leak while we are in there.

included.title: Transmission service
included.detail: Proper fluid and filter service on automatics and DSG/dual-clutch boxes, including the sealed 'lifetime' units that genuinely need a change. ZF-style 8-speeds and BMW autos last far longer when they are actually serviced.

included.title: Brake fluid flush
included.detail: Brake fluid absorbs water and degrades. We do a full flush to the correct DOT spec, which protects pedal feel and your ABS and stability-control hardware. Recommended roughly every two years on most European cars.

included.title: Coolant service
included.detail: Drain and refill with the manufacturer-approved coolant only. Mixing the wrong coolant chemistry causes corrosion and clogged passages, and on these engines that is an expensive mistake.

included.title: Power steering, differential and transfer case
included.detail: Often forgotten and rarely cheap to ignore. We service power steering fluid, differential gear oil, and the transfer-case fluid on all-wheel-drive BMW, Audi, Mercedes and Land Rover models to keep them quiet and reliable.

included.title: An honest service plan
included.detail: We tell you what your specific model and mileage actually need now, what can wait, and what the 'lifetime fluid' label is quietly costing you. No padded menus.

signals: Oil-change reminder or service light is on
It has been over two years since your last brake fluid flush
Gear changes feel harsh, slow, or are slipping
Coolant looks dirty, rusty, or is low
Power steering whines or the all-wheel drive feels notchy in tight turns
services.whyEuropean: A general shop or quick-lube stocks one or two oils and treats every car the same. European engines do not allow that. A BMW that takes a specific long-life synthetic will suffer on a generic 5W-30, and the wrong coolant or a 'sealed' transmission left untouched is how these cars die early. We carry and use the exact approved fluids for each marque, we know which lifetime claims are marketing, and we have done it on these cars for 30 years. With the nearest comparable European specialist over 100 miles away, that knowledge is worth the local call.

process.step: Call and tell us your car
process.detail: Phone (318) 445-6007 with your make, model, year and mileage. We schedule you in and confirm exactly which fluids your car is due for. No online forms, just talk to us.
We use the manufacturer-approved fluid for every job and inspect the common leak and wear points for your model while the car is up, so nothing gets missed.
We tell you what we did, what we saw, and when each fluid is next due. Clear pricing, no surprises.

faqs.q: Does my 'lifetime' transmission really need a service?
faqs.a: Usually, yes. 'Lifetime' often means the warranty period, not the life of the car. Many sealed automatics and dual-clutch boxes last far longer with a proper fluid and filter service. Call us with your model and we will tell you straight.

faqs.q: Why not just use any synthetic oil?
faqs.a: Because European engines call for specific factory approvals, not just a viscosity number. The wrong oil can accelerate wear and, on some engines, contribute to timing chain and tensioner problems. We use the approved spec for your car, every time.

faqs.q: How often should I flush the brake fluid?
faqs.a: On most European cars, roughly every two years regardless of mileage. Brake fluid absorbs moisture over time, which lowers its boiling point and can corrode expensive ABS components. It is cheap insurance.

services.metaTitle: Brake & Suspension Repair | Tony's European Auto, Alexandria
services.metaDescription: European brake and suspension repair in Alexandria, LA. Pads, rotors, struts, control arms, alignment, and the precise handling restored. Call (318) 445-6007.
services.tagline: The stopping power and the planted, confident handling your European car was built to have, restored the right way.
intro: A European car is engineered to brake hard and stay composed through a corner. When that goes away, you feel it: a steering wheel that shudders under your hands, a brake pedal that pulses, a clunk over every expansion joint, a car that wanders instead of tracking straight. Those are not things to live with.

We service the full chassis: pads, rotors, calipers, and brake lines through to struts, shocks, control arms, bushings, ball joints, wheel bearings, and alignment. We use parts that meet the original specification rather than the cheapest box on the shelf, because the wrong friction material or a soft aftermarket bushing changes how the whole car feels. The goal is simple: make it stop and steer the way the factory intended.

included.title: Brake pads & rotors
included.detail: Quality-matched pads and rotors that quiet the squeal and kill the pulsation, with brake systems bedded in properly so they perform from the first stop.

included.title: Calipers & brake lines
included.detail: Sticking calipers, dragging pads, and aging flex lines diagnosed and replaced so braking is even, firm, and predictable instead of pulling to one side.

included.title: Struts, shocks & springs
included.detail: Worn dampers that let the car float, bounce, or crash over bumps replaced to restore the controlled, settled ride a German or British chassis is known for.

included.title: Control arms, bushings & ball joints
included.detail: The links that locate your wheels wear out and introduce clunks, vague steering, and uneven tire wear. We replace them and tighten the front end back up.

included.title: Wheel bearings
included.detail: That droning hum that rises with speed is usually a bearing. We pinpoint the corner and replace it before it leaves you stranded or damages the hub.

included.title: Alignment
included.detail: After suspension work, the car gets aligned to factory specification so it tracks straight, the wheel sits centered, and your tires stop wearing on one edge.

signals: Steering wheel or brake pedal shudders when you slow down
Squealing, grinding, or a metallic scrape when braking
Clunks or rattles over bumps and rough pavement
The car drifts or pulls to one side on a straight road
Uneven tire wear or a humming noise that grows with speed
services.whyEuropean: A European chassis is a tuned system, not a collection of generic parts. The pad compound, the bushing durometer, and the alignment tolerances were chosen to deliver a specific feel, and a general shop that bolts on whatever fits will hand you back a car that stops and steers like something else. We know how a BMW front end should feel tight, how a Mercedes should ride settled, and how a Porsche should turn in, so we fix the right component to the right spec instead of guessing. The nearest comparable European specialist is more than 100 miles away.

process.detail: Phone us at (318) 445-6007 and tell us what you feel and hear: the pulse, the clunk, the pull. We book you in. There is no online form, just a real conversation.

process.step: We drive it and inspect it
process.detail: We road test the car to feel the symptom for ourselves, then put it on the lift to inspect brakes, suspension joints, and tires, and tell you exactly what is worn and what is not.
We quote the work plainly before we start, replace what needs replacing with spec-correct parts, align it if needed, and hand back a car that stops and steers the way it should.

faqs.q: My brakes only squeal sometimes. Is it still worth a look?
faqs.a: Yes. Intermittent squeal can be light surface glazing or early pad wear, but it can also be a wear indicator doing its job or a caliper starting to drag. It is a quick inspection and far cheaper to catch now than after it scores a rotor.

faqs.q: Do I need an alignment after suspension work?
faqs.a: Almost always. Replacing control arms, struts, or other steering and suspension parts changes the geometry, so an alignment to factory specification is what makes the car track straight again and keeps your tires from wearing unevenly.

faqs.q: Can you use original-quality parts, not just cheap aftermarket?
faqs.a: Yes, and we prefer to. On European cars the friction material and bushing stiffness directly affect how the car feels, so we fit parts that meet the original specification rather than the cheapest option that happens to bolt on.

services.metaTitle: Engine Repair for European Cars | Tony's, Alexandria LA
services.metaDescription: European engine repair in Alexandria, LA. Timing chains, oil and coolant leaks, misfires, turbo and check-engine fixes by techs who know these engines. Call (318) 445-6007.
services.tagline: Timing, leaks, cooling, misfires, and turbo work done right by people who know these specific engines.
intro: European engines are precise, and they fail in specific, predictable ways. A BMW that's due for a timing chain guide, a Mercedes seeping oil from the filter housing, an Audi stumbling under load: these are pattern problems, and the fix depends on knowing the engine before you ever pull a cover. That's the difference between a real repair and an expensive guess.

We've worked on BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Porsche, Audi, Volkswagen, Land Rover, Jaguar, and the rest for 30 years here in Alexandria. We diagnose the actual cause, quote it straight, and use the right parts and procedures for your car. Dealer-grade capability, with local pricing and a family that answers the phone.

included.title: Timing chains and belts
included.detail: Worn chain guides and tensioners (a known weak point on many BMW and VW/Audi engines), stretched chains, and interval timing-belt service before a failure bends valves. We replace the supporting parts, not just the chain.

included.title: Oil and coolant leaks
included.detail: Valve cover gaskets, the Mercedes oil filter housing gasket, oil cooler seals, water pumps, and thermostat housings. We trace the leak to its source instead of cleaning it up and hoping.

included.title: Cooling system repair
included.detail: Plastic coolant components on European engines get brittle and crack with age. We handle water pumps, thermostats, expansion tanks, hoses, and electric fans before an overheat turns into head-gasket damage.

included.title: Gaskets and seals
included.detail: Head gaskets, intake and exhaust manifold gaskets, crankshaft and camshaft seals. Done with proper torque specs and sequence so the repair holds.

included.title: Misfires and rough running
included.detail: Coils, plugs, injectors, vacuum and intake leaks, carbon buildup on direct-injection intake valves (common on Audi and BMW), and timing-related misfires. We find which cylinder and why.

included.title: Turbo and check-engine causes
included.detail: Boost leaks, wastegate and actuator faults, failing turbos, and the stored codes behind a check-engine light. We read live data and test components instead of throwing parts at it.

signals: Oil spots or a burning-oil smell after parking
Coolant loss, overheating, or a sweet smell from the engine
Check-engine light, flashing light, or rough idle
Rattle or chain noise at cold startup
Loss of power, hesitation, or a whistle under acceleration
services.whyEuropean: European engines don't tolerate generic repair. The torque sequences, the special tools, the plastic cooling parts that look fine and aren't, the carbon that builds on direct-injection valves: a general shop often misses these or learns on your car. We've seen the same engines and the same failures for three decades, so we know where to look first and how to fix it so it stays fixed. The nearest comparable European specialist is more than 100 miles away.

process.detail: Reach us at (318) 445-6007 and tell us the symptom, the model, and what you're hearing or seeing. We'll let you know what to expect and get you in. No forms, no portal, just a real conversation.
We inspect, pull codes, read live data, and confirm the source before quoting. You get a clear explanation and an honest price, not a parts-cannon.
Correct parts, correct procedures, and a car that runs the way it should. We tell you what's urgent and what can wait.

faqs.q: Do you work on my specific make and model?
faqs.a: Almost certainly. We service BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Porsche, Audi, Land Rover, Jaguar, Volkswagen, Maserati, MINI, and classic air-cooled VWs and British roadsters. Call with your year and model and we'll confirm.

faqs.q: Can you do engine work for less than the dealership?
faqs.a: Yes. We have dealer-level capability without dealer overhead, so you get the right diagnosis and repair at local-shop pricing. We'll quote your specific job straight before any work starts.

faqs.q: My check-engine light is on but the car drives fine. Should I bring it in?
faqs.a: Worth a call. Some codes are minor, but a misfire, a small coolant leak, or a timing fault can quietly cause expensive damage. We'll read it and tell you whether it's urgent or can wait.

services.metaTitle: Classic & Exotic Car Care | Tony's European, Alexandria
services.metaDescription: Careful, knowledgeable hands for air-cooled VWs, British roadsters, and modern exotics. Preservation-minded service in Alexandria, LA. Call (318) 445-6007.
services.tagline: The cars most shops won't touch, handled by people who actually want to.
intro: Some cars are not just transportation. An air-cooled Beetle, a British roadster, a Lamborghini or Maserati: these need hands that understand what they are and what they should stay. Too many shops either turn them away or learn on your dime. We do neither.

For 30 years the Boatwright family has worked on European machinery, and that experience carries straight into the classic and exotic side of the bench. We treat these cars the way their owners do: with patience, the right approach, and respect for originality. The nearest comparable European specialist is more than 100 miles away, which means careful work on a rare car no longer has to mean a long tow.

included.title: Air-cooled VW service
included.detail: Beetles, Buses, and other air-cooled VWs: valve adjustments, carburetor and timing work, points and dwell, oil-bath and cooling tin details that modern shops simply don't see anymore.

included.title: British roadster work
included.detail: Austin-Healey, MG, and friends. Carburetor tuning, ignition, cooling, electrical gremlins, and the patient sorting these cars reward rather than the parts-cannon approach that ruins them.

included.title: Modern exotic maintenance
included.detail: Lamborghini, Maserati, and Porsche scheduled service and repair: fluids, brakes, belts and chains, and diagnostics handled with the care a six-figure car deserves.

included.title: Preservation-minded repair
included.detail: We favor keeping a car correct and original where it makes sense, and we tell you plainly when a part should be preserved, rebuilt, or replaced.

included.title: Honest condition assessment
included.detail: Buying, selling, or just wondering what you have. We will look the car over and give you a straight read on its mechanical condition and what it needs.

included.title: Pre-storage and recommissioning
included.detail: Getting a seasonal or long-stored car ready to sit, or carefully waking one back up so it runs right instead of running rough.

signals: A classic that sat all winter and now runs rough or won't start
Carburetors that need balancing, tuning, or a careful rebuild
An exotic due for scheduled service you don't trust to a general shop
Oil leaks, overheating, or electrical faults on an older car
You were quoted a long tow because no local shop will touch it
services.whyEuropean: Classic and exotic European cars punish guesswork. An air-cooled VW timed by feel, a roadster's carburetors balanced by ear, a Porsche serviced to the right spec: these are not jobs you hand to whoever is open. General shops often lack both the knowledge and the patience these cars demand, and a wrong move on something rare or original is expensive and sometimes permanent. A specialist who has spent decades inside European machinery brings the judgment to know what to fix, what to leave alone, and how to keep the car true to itself.

process.step: Call and tell us about the car
process.detail: Phone (318) 445-6007. There are no online forms here. We would rather hear what you have, what it's doing, and what you want from it.
We look the car over, talk through originality and goals, and lay out a plan with honest options rather than assuming you want everything new.
We do the work with the patience these cars need and keep you informed. No surprises, no parts you didn't agree to.

faqs.q: Do you really work on air-cooled VWs and old British cars?
faqs.a: Yes. These are exactly the cars we mean by classic care. Air-cooled Beetles and Buses, Austin-Healeys, MGs, and similar roadsters are all welcome. They need a different skill set than modern cars, and we have it.

faqs.q: Will you keep my car original?
faqs.a: Where it makes sense, yes. We are preservation-minded and prefer to keep a car correct rather than modernize it without reason. We will always tell you plainly when a part is better preserved, rebuilt, or replaced, and let you decide.

faqs.q: My exotic is due for service but I don't trust the dealer drive. Can you help?
faqs.a: Yes. We service Porsche, Maserati, Lamborghini, and other exotics to spec, with the care a car like that deserves and without the long round trip. Call us and we'll talk through what your car needs.

services.metaTitle: Pre-Purchase Inspections & Warranty Repair | Tony's European
services.metaDescription: Buying a used BMW, Mercedes, Porsche or Audi near Alexandria LA? Get an honest pre-purchase inspection and warranty repair from a European specialist. Call to book.
services.tagline: Know exactly what you are buying, and have your warranty claims handled by people who know these cars.
intro: A used European car can be the best deal in the lot or the most expensive mistake of your year, and the difference is rarely visible on a test drive. A pre-purchase inspection puts the car on our lift, on our scan tools, and in the hands of people who fix these exact problems every week.

We also welcome extended-warranty and third-party-warranty work. If you bought a policy, we will inspect the failure, document it the way the administrator needs, and get you back on the road. No drama, no upsell, just a straight answer on what the car actually needs.

included.title: Full Pre-Purchase Inspection (PPI)
included.detail: A road test plus a lift inspection covering engine, transmission, brakes, suspension, steering, cooling, leaks, tires, and underbody. We tell you what is wrong, what is coming, and roughly what it costs to fix.

included.title: Computer Scan & Stored Faults
included.detail: We pull codes from every module, not just the engine light. Cleared codes, pending faults, and a history of resets often reveal what a seller would rather you not see.

included.title: Model-Specific Problem Checks
included.detail: We look where these cars actually fail: BMW timing-chain and VANOS wear, Mercedes oil-filter-housing and valve-cover leaks, Audi and VW carbon buildup and timing components, Porsche IMS bearing and bore-scoring concerns on the applicable years, Land Rover air-suspension and oil leaks.

included.title: Honest Go / No-Go Guidance
included.detail: You get a plain-English recommendation: buy it, walk away, or use it as leverage to negotiate. We have no stake in the sale, so the advice is yours alone.

included.title: Extended & Third-Party Warranty Repair
included.detail: We diagnose the failure, photograph and document it, and work with your warranty administrator so covered repairs get approved and completed correctly.

included.title: Leak, Fluid & Service-History Review
included.detail: We check for the slow leaks and skipped services that turn a clean-looking car into a money pit, and tell you whether the maintenance was really kept up.

signals: You found a used European car and want an unbiased opinion before you sign
The seller says it 'just needs a small repair' and you want the truth
Dashboard warning lights that the seller cleared or cannot explain
Your extended-warranty company needs a shop to diagnose and document a claim
A dealer or private seller is rushing you to buy today
services.whyEuropean: A general used-car inspection checks fluids and kicks the tires. It will not catch a BMW timing chain stretching, a Mercedes oil-filter housing weeping, carbon-clogged Audi intake valves, or a Porsche with the wrong IMS bearing for its year. Those are the failures that cost thousands, and they are exactly what a European specialist is trained to find. We work on these marques every day, so we know the difference between normal age and an expensive problem hiding under a fresh detail job.

process.detail: Phone us at (318) 445-6007 with the car's year, make, model, and mileage. We will tell you what the inspection covers and set a time. There is no online form: a quick call gets it scheduled.
Bring the car in, or arrange with the seller to drop it off. We road test it, put it on the lift, scan every module, and check the known weak points for that model.
We walk you through what we found, rank it by urgency and cost, and give a clear go or no-go. For warranty work, we handle the claim paperwork and the repair.

faqs.q: How long does a pre-purchase inspection take?
faqs.a: Most take a few hours once the car is in our hands. Call ahead so we can reserve a lift and have a technician ready. If the seller can drop the car off in the morning, we can usually have answers for you the same day.

faqs.q: Can you do the inspection if the car is at a dealership or with a private seller?
faqs.a: Yes. The car needs to come to our shop at 3800 McKeithen Drive so we can put it on the lift and scan it properly. Many sellers will allow a buyer to bring the car in for an inspection, and we are glad to coordinate the timing with you over the phone.

faqs.q: Do you really work with extended and third-party warranties?
faqs.a: We do. Bring your policy details and we will diagnose the failure, document it the way your administrator requires, and complete any covered repair. We will also tell you honestly when something is not likely to be covered so there are no surprises.
